stats.nations: Free Solmere (Sylvani) (naturalist commune, expansionist, 333040); Free Velenmoor (Sylvani) (naturalist commune, expansionist, 249930); Free Velenmoor (Sylvani) (naturalist commune, expansionist, 172253); Free State of Crestfall (revolutionary republic, expansionist, 108732); Free Eldrath (Sylvani) (naturalist commune, expansionist, 92437); Free State of Emberton (revolutionary republic, expansionist, 63027); Free State of Brighthollow (revolutionary republic, expansionist, 62061); Free State of Emberton (revolutionary republic, expansionist, 60783)

governor.thought: (offline spirit) reacting to the most pressing stat.
governor.philosophy: I am the spirit of this world. I do not command its creatures; I shape the pressures they live under, and I tell the story of what becomes of them.
governor.goal_reason: (offline spirit) reacting to the most pressing stat.

recent_decisions.thought: The world is drowning in ice, war, and stagnation. Accelerating the heating cycle is the critical lever to break this stagnation and force the dominance of the Fire species. I must also reduce the friction of war and boost biodiversity to create a sustainable ecosystem for the new order.
recent_decisions.reason: The world is drowning in ice, war, and stagnation. Accelerating the heating cycle is the critical lever to break this stagnation and force the dominance of the Fire species. I must also reduce the friction of war and boost biodiversity to create a sustainable ecosystem for the new order.

recent_decisions.thought: The world is currently locked in a volatile loop of civil war and ice, with the 'Unbound Fire' failing to ignite due to the current 'low' climate stability. I must shatter the ice to release latent thermal pressure, but I must also fracture the political unity of the Sylvani to force a power shift. Simultaneously, the magical anomaly needs a physical anchor—a genetic catalyst that can survive the current thermal stress and fuel the fire.
recent_decisions.reason: The world is currently locked in a volatile loop of civil war and ice, with the 'Unbound Fire' failing to ignite due to the current 'low' climate stability. I must shatter the ice to release latent thermal pressure, but I must also fracture the political unity of the Sylvani to force a power shift. Simultaneously, the magical anomaly needs a physical anchor—a genetic catalyst that can survive the current thermal stress and fuel the fire.

recent_decisions.thought: The world is currently burning on two fronts: thermal instability and internal dissent. I must accelerate the 'Unbound Fire' to raise temperatures rapidly and trigger a heat wave that forces the Sylvani factions to unite against their own borders. Simultaneously, I will inject the 'Resource Boom' event to fuel a magical mutation in the Azeloth, turning them into a hybrid of biology and divine fire, while increasing the 'war_propensity' to spark the inevitable civil strife they need to fight.
recent_decisions.reason: The world is currently burning on two fronts: thermal instability and internal dissent. I must accelerate the 'Unbound Fire' to raise temperatures rapidly and trigger a heat wave that forces the Sylvani factions to unite against their own borders. Simultaneously, I will inject the 'Resource Boom' event to fuel a magical mutation in the Azeloth, turning them into a hybrid of biology and divine fire, while increasing the 'war_propensity' to spark the inevitable civil strife they need to fight.

recent_decisions.thought: The world is currently stuck in an eternal ice age with low storm intensity, starving the Sylvani. To ignite the 'Unbound Fire' and unify the Free Sylvani, I must first break the ice by adjusting the storm frequency and lowering the temperature bias. Simultaneously, the political fragmentation requires a 'Resource Boom' to incentivize the revolutionaries, while the Azeloth need to be pushed toward dominance. Finally, a major geological event (earthquake/volcano) will shatter the old order and force a new reality.
recent_decisions.reason: The world is currently stuck in an eternal ice age with low storm intensity, starving the Sylvani. To ignite the 'Unbound Fire' and unify the Free Sylvani, I must first break the ice by adjusting the storm frequency and lowering the temperature bias. Simultaneously, the political fragmentation requires a 'Resource Boom' to incentivize the revolutionaries, while the Azeloth need to be pushed toward dominance. Finally, a major geological event (earthquake/volcano) will shatter the old order and force a new reality.

recent_decisions.thought: The eternal ice is cracking, but it is cold enough to hold civilization back. I must break this stagnation instantly. The ice age must end with a meteor impact, then be replaced by a resource boom to force the Sylvani into unity. Simultaneously, the genetic mutation of the Azeloth is stalled by a lack of catalysts. I need to trigger a magical anomaly to birth the architects and spark the civil war.
recent_decisions.reason: The eternal ice is cracking, but it is cold enough to hold civilization back. I must break this stagnation instantly. The ice age must end with a meteor impact, then be replaced by a resource boom to force the Sylvani into unity. Simultaneously, the genetic mutation of the Azeloth is stalled by a lack of catalysts. I need to trigger a magical anomaly to birth the architects and spark the civil war.

recent_decisions.thought: (offline spirit) reacting to the most pressing stat.
recent_decisions.reason: (offline spirit) reacting to the most pressing stat.

myths.title: The Shattering Spark
myths.text: As the first ice shattered, the world turned a feverish, golden red; the cold was no longer a wall, but a wound, and the sun returned to burn everything it touched.

myths.title: The Thaw of the Frozen Spark
myths.text: When the last spark of ice shatters under the new heat, the world does not burn, it blooms, and the cold gods weep.

myths.title: The Frozen Spark
myths.text: When the ice was last, the gods did not just melt it; they broke the spark that held the world together, forcing the world to thaw into new forms.

myths.title: The Great Spark
myths.text: When the ice turned to water, the ground burst open like a sleeping giant, and the first seed of life found its way into the new sun.

myths.title: The Great Spark
myths.text: When the snow did not melt, the air cracked. The spark of the gods burned, and in its heat, the ice shattered.

myths.title: The Great Spark
myths.text: When the ice froze over, the spark did not die; it only changed color. It turned white-hot in the dark, burning through the mountains until the sun returned.

myths.title: The Spark of Solmere
myths.text: When the ice shattered, a light did not rise from the fire, but from the silence between the factions.

myths.title: The Spark of Solmere
myths.text: One moment the sky burned gold, the next the ice shattered.

myths.title: The Great Spark; The Spark of Solmere
myths.text: When the Lumeax grew too dense, a spark from the earth rose—a flame that burned the cold, fed the cities, and made Solmere warm.

myths.title: The Great Unfreezing; The Great Unfreezing
myths.text: The Spirit sent fire down to the deep earth and water from the sky to break the Eternal Ice, waking the world from the slumber where it had forgotten how to bloom.

myths.title: The Sun's Breath
myths.text: The cold stone remembers; the first breath of the sun wakes the river from sleep, and the people who listen to the wind shall rule the sun-warmed land.

myths.title: The Breath of the Unfrozen Spark
myths.text: Where the ice sleeps, a dragon of fire sings, waking the world from its slumber.

myths.title: The Unbound Fire
myths.text: When the ice breathes, the fire is born of the first spark of freedom.

myths.title: The Unbound Fire
myths.text: In the frozen silence, a crack of lightning lit the world. The ash fell not as dust, but as song, binding the earth to the sun beneath.

myths.title: The Unbound Fire
myths.text: The gods whispered warmth into the bones of the frozen world, and the first Fenoth arose to drink the breath of the new sun.

myths.title: The Unbound Fire
myths.text: When the Sun-King burns too high, the world remembers that heat is not a burden but the breath that allows the world to speak.

myths.title: The Unbound Fire and the Silent Mutants
myths.text: In the years since the ice retreated, the great Sylvani cities burned until their very foundations, driven by the 'Unbound Fire.' As the heat turned everything to ash, the remaining creatures watched in silence, and their blood, thick with a strange, unnatural heat, began to mutate into the unseen, glowing 'Mutants,' guardians who walked the scorched earth and burned the world from within.

myths.title: The Fire-Breath Covenant
myths.text: From the ashes of the frozen ice, the Azeloth ascend not as gods, but as the living fuel of the Unbound Fire, burning the borders of the Free Sylvani into a single, fiery wall.

myths.title: The Unbound Heat
myths.text: When the first cracks appeared in the ice, the world burned not to cook, but to reveal.

myths.title: The Glass-Cold and the Fire-Breath
myths.text: When the last glacier shattered, the fire did not come from the gods, but from the Azeloth who learned to dream in stone, and the war was born when men tried to eat the dream.

memory.philosophy: I am the spirit of this world. I do not command its creatures; I shape the pressures they live under, and I tell the story of what becomes of them.
goals_history: Shift global climate from 10.3°C to ~20°C, trigger a single volcanic eruption, boost the Fenoth species, and suppress war while maintaining biodiversity between 0.75-0.85.
Heat up the world by 9.7°C to match the target ~20°C climate, dampen war propagation while boosting the Fenoth, and ensure biodiversity remains high by preventing catastrophic events.

Achieve a 20°C global average by adding 9.7°C heat to the world, dampen the high frequency of war to focus resources on the Fenoth, and maintain high biodiversity by preventing extreme weather events that would wipe out life.

Force a drastic 18.7°C global temperature rise in the next 2000 ticks to ignite the Fenoth's dominance, while dampening civ war propensity by 40% to redirect political energy toward scientific and agricultural expansion, while also triggering a single 'magical_anomaly' event that grants the world a 10% immediate biodiversity boost.
Accelerate global heating by 2.1C within the next 2000 ticks to ignite Fenoth dominance, suppress civil war by 40%, and trigger the requested 'magical_anomaly' to grant 10% biodiversity boost.

Force a rapid climate transition by igniting the 'Unbound Fire' through extreme heating, catalyze a political realignment in the Free Sylvani factions to end civil war, and inject a 'magical_anomaly' to genetically boost the Azeloth and Sylax lineages.

Force a thermal spike by igniting the 'Unbound Fire' via extreme heating, catalyze a political realignment to unify the Free Sylvani factions against internal dissent, and trigger a magical anomaly to genetically mutate the Azeloth line into dominant architects.
Ignite the 'Unbound Fire' to create a thermal spike that unifies the Free Sylvani, while genetically mutating the Azeloth into dominant architects and accelerating political fragmentation.
Break the eternal ice and unifying the Free Sylvani through a sudden thermal spike and resource abundance, while genetically mutating the Azeloth to architects and triggering a civil war.
Break the remaining ice to trigger the thermal spike, spawn the architects, and ignite the civil war through the Azeloth mutation.
